title: chloropyron maritimum subsp. parryi
intro: Chloropyron maritimum subsp. parryi, also known as the Coastal Sweetpea, is a perennial plant native to the coastal regions of the Pacific Northwest in North America, characterized by its fragrant white flowers and hairy leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Chloropyron maritimum subsp. parryi include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
